paper.title_en: Prototype assembly and tests for CEPC vertex detector
paper.abstract_en: The first four layers of the Circular Electron Positron Collider (CEPC) Vertex Detector (VTXD) are designed using wafer-scale sensors fabricated through stitching technology. To position the innermost layer as close as possible to the central beam pipe, the design radius is set to 11.1 mm, which poses significant challenges in the development of the detector modules. In this study, a prototype module was developed to validate the key techniques required for bent sensors. Additionally, to evaluate the performance of silicon pixel sensors after bending, a small-area bent detector module with a bending radius of 20 mm was fabricated using 50μm-thick Monolithic Active Pixel Sensors (MAPS). Laboratory tests showed that the mechanical bending did not cause observable degradation in the electrical functionality of the sensors. Furthermore, beam tests conducted with a 1.3 GeV electron beam demonstrated that most areas of the bent chip maintains a spatial resolution comparable to the approximately 6.8μm resolution measured for the planar chip. These results confirm the operational reliability of bent MAPS, providing a crucial experimental foundation for future development of ultra-light cylindrical vertex detectors.
